claim: Semantic caching uses vector embeddings to identify similar queries and reuse cached responses, reducing redundant LLM calls and latency compared to traditional exact-match caching
actionable: Implement semantic caching by embedding queries and performing nearest-neighbor search against cached queries to identify similar requests.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Semantic caching introduces a risk of false positives where similar but non-equivalent questions reuse answers - this requires tuning a distance threshold and treating caching like a machine learning classification problem
actionable: Monitor precision and recall for your semantic cache; tune the similarity distance threshold to balance cache hits against false positives.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Semantic caching can reduce annual inference costs by 30-40% at enterprise scale - an example showed reducing $39k to $27k annually with 36% cache hit rate
actionable: Calculate ROI for semantic caching in your system; typical savings range from 30-40% of inference costs depending on query overlap.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Evaluate semantic caching effectiveness using precision, recall, and F1 score (not just cache hit rate) - treat it as a classification problem with performance monitoring
actionable: Track precision (cache quality), recall (cache coverage), and F1 score for your semantic cache; adjust distance threshold based on these metrics.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Production semantic caching often uses a multi-layer approach: embedding-based vector cache + fuzzy matching + code detection filters + temporal context detection
actionable: Implement a layered caching system starting with vector search, then adding fuzzy matching, and special handling for code/time-sensitive queries.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Inference costs (token processing) have become the dominant unit cost in LLM systems, not data engineering or training infrastructure
actionable: Optimize for inference cost reduction first; implement techniques like semantic caching and token-efficient tool use before addressing other bottlenecks.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: For agentic systems, context window grows with each loop (stateless agents resend context), making semantic caching particularly valuable to reduce redundant computation
actionable: Prioritize semantic caching for agentic workflows since agents repeatedly rebuild context in loops.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications

claim: Semantic cache performance should be monitored for distribution drift over time - cached patterns that work well today may become stale as queries evolve with product changes
actionable: Periodically review and retune your semantic cache distance thresholds as query patterns and product FAQs evolve.
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nitin Kanukolanu: Semantic Caching for LLM Applications
